info.title: Agentic Platform - Eval Service
info.description:
## Overview

The Eval Service provides a robust, scalable, and flexible evaluation platform for testing and measuring the performance of AI agents and retrieval systems. It implements a decoupled, API-first architecture with an orchestration layer (API service for managing definitions and runs) and an execution layer (asynchronous worker engine for executing evaluations).

This design separates lightweight, synchronous tasks (like creating a dataset) from heavyweight, asynchronous tasks (like running 500 test cases), ensuring the platform remains responsive and scalable.

## Getting Started

**PRIOR TO USING THIS API, YOU MUST AGREE TO OUR TERMS OF SERVICE**

The API is protected by OAuth2.0 and requires a valid TID access token in the `Authorization` header. Refer to [TID Authentication Guide](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/authentication) for details.

## Capabilities Overview

* **Dataset Management**
  * Organize test cases (data set records) into datasets
  * Link datasets to KB Service libraries for access control and inheritance
  * Support both agent testing (messages) and retrieval testing (query) records
  * Flexible metadata for categorization, filtering, and tracing

* **data set Records**
  * Unified test case model supporting multiple evaluation types:
    * **Agent records**: Multi-turn conversations with `messages` array
    * **Retrieval records**: Simple search queries with `query` string
  * **Multi-modal support**: Text, images, audio, video in messages and outputs
  * Define expected values: outputs, context chunks (with relevance), tool calls
  * Capture actual values: outputs and tools from reference runs for regression testing
  * Reference outputs: Store "blessed" outputs for baseline comparison

* **Evaluator Management (Agent-Like Entities)**
  * **System Evaluators**: Trimble-managed templates (read-only, must clone to use)
    * Pre-optimized for common metrics (faithfulness, recall, safety, relevance)
    * Clone via `POST /evaluators/{template_id}/clone` to create user-owned instances
  * **User Evaluators**: Custom LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators or cloned instances
    * Full CRUD operations (create, update, delete)
    * Customizable prompts, metrics, and configurations
  * **Access Control**: Same permission model as agents.yaml
    * `admins`: Manage evaluator config and permissions
    * `users`: Create and view jobs with evaluator
    * Actor claims: users:, accounts:, applications:
  * **Agent Permissions**: Define which agents evaluator can invoke for testing
  * **Execution Configuration**: Model selection, timeout, retry logic
  * **Multi-modal evaluation**: Assess image quality, audio clarity, video analysis, TTS
  * Multi-metric output from single evaluator execution
  * Support for boolean, numerical, and categorical metrics

* **Evaluation Jobs**
  * Asynchronous execution of evaluators against datasets
  * Top-level resource: `/jobs` with `evaluator_id` in payload
  * Easy filtering: by evaluator, dataset, agent, status, or type
  * **Job Queuing**: FIFO queue ensures sequential execution per agent/evaluator/dataset combination
  * **Job Statuses**: `QUEUED` → `PENDING` → `RUNNING` → `COMPLETED`/`FAILED`/`CANCELLED`
  * **Job Cancellation**: Cancel queued jobs via `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel` (admins only)
  * **Agent Eval**: End-to-end testing of complete agent behavior with full context
  * **Retriever Eval**: Component-level testing of KB search quality in isolation
  * **Multi-modal testing**: Vision models, audio models, image generation, TTS, video
  * Programmatic success criteria with pass/fail outcomes
  * Progress tracking (total, completed, failed records)
  * Creates individual `RecordEvaluation` entries per record with `job_id` link
  * Aggregated summary results and detailed per-record results

* **Individual Record Evaluations**
  * Unified storage for both LLM and human evaluations per record
  * Endpoint: `/datasets/{dataset_id}/records/{record_id}/evaluations`
  * Filter by source type, evaluator, or specific human actor
  * **Inter-rater reliability analysis**: Compare human vs LLM scores across same records
    * Calculate agreement metrics (MAE, correlation, Cohen's Kappa)
    * Identify high-disagreement records for review
    * Validate LLM evaluator quality against human judgment
  * Link evaluations back to jobs via `job_id`
  * Support for evaluation comments and metadata
  * Delete/update human evaluations (LLM evaluations immutable)

## Common API Concepts

This API uses HTTPS requests with JSON-formatted content in requests and responses.

### Cross-Origin Resource Sharing

This API features Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) implemented in compliance with the
[W3C spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/cors/) that allows cross-domain communication from the browser.

### Date Format

All dates in the API use UTC and are strings in the [ISO 8601 combined date and time representation
format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Combined_date_and_time_representations)
Example `2015-05-15T15:50:38.311Z`.

### Paginated Responses

Endpoints that return collections support pagination using cursor-based pagination.
Responses include a `pagination` object with `next_cursor` (for fetching the next page) and `total` (total count of items).

Common query parameters:
- `page` - Page number (default: 1)
- `limit` - Number of items per page (default: 100)
- `tag` - Filter by metadata tag

## Access Control Model

The Eval Service uses a role-based access control model similar to agents.yaml:

### Evaluator Permissions

Evaluators have two roles defined using actor claims:
- **Admin Role**: Can manage evaluator configuration, update rubrics, manage permissions, delete evaluator, and create jobs
- **User Role**: Can create and view jobs with this evaluator

**Actor Claims** use the same syntax as agents.yaml:
- `users:123456789` - Specific user by TID
- `accounts:qa-team` - All users in an account
- `applications:eval-bot` - Specific application
- `users:*` or `applications:*` - All authenticated users/applications
- `accounts:trimble-placeholder-of-employees` - All Trimble employees

### Dataset Permissions

Datasets inherit permissions from KB Service:
- **Dataset Creation/Update**: Users must have read access to all KB libraries listed in `kb_library_links`
- **Record Management**: Requires access to dataset's linked KB libraries

### Job Execution Permissions

Job creation requires:
- Evaluator must be user-owned (`is_system_managed: false`)
- User must be in evaluator's `users` or `admins` list
- User must have access to dataset's KB libraries
- **External permissions** (managed in other services):
  - For AGENT jobs: Evaluator's `created_by` actor must have access to target agent (in Agent Service)
  - KB libraries must be shared with evaluator's `created_by` actor (in KB Service)

### System Evaluators (Templates)

System evaluators are read-only templates:
- Managed by Trimble (`accounts:trimble-placeholder-of-employees`)
- Cannot be modified or deleted by users
- **Cannot be used directly for job creation**
- Must be **cloned** via `POST /evaluators/{evaluator_id}/clone` to create a user-owned copy
- Cloned evaluators inherit the rubric but allow custom permissions and configuration

**Workflow:**
1. Browse system evaluators: `GET /evaluators?is_system_managed=true`
2. Find desired template by name (e.g., "Faithfulness Evaluator")
3. Clone the template: `POST /evaluators/{template_id}/clone`
4. **(External)** Grant evaluator access to agents in Agent Service and KB libraries in KB Service
5. Create jobs: `POST /jobs` with the evaluator_id

## Data Model

### Datasets

A "Dataset" is a top-level collection that groups data set records for a specific evaluation purpose (e.g., "Support Bot Regression Tests"). Datasets link to KB Service libraries and inherit their permission model.

### data set Records

A "data set Record" is a unified test case within a dataset. It supports both agent evaluation and retrieval evaluation:

**For CONVERSATION records (agent testing):**
- **messages**: Array of user/assistant turns (conversation history)
- **expected_output**: The ideal agent response (ground truth)
- **expected_context**: (Optional) Expected KB chunks the agent should use
- **expected_tools**: (Optional) Expected tool calls

**For QUERY records (retrieval testing):**
- **query**: Simple search query string
- **expected_context**: Expected chunks that should be retrieved (with relevance levels)

**Both types support:**
- **Actual values**: Captured results from reference runs (actual_output, actual_tools)
- **Reference output**: A "blessed" output for regression testing
- **Flexible metadata**: For categorization, filtering, and tracing

**Expected vs Actual vs Reference:**
- `expected_*` - The ideal/correct behavior (ground truth for scoring)
- `actual_*` - What a specific run actually produced (captured for comparison)
- `reference_output` - A previously approved "good" output (for regression tests)

**Note:** A record must have either `messages` OR `query`, determined by `record_type`:
- `record_type: CONVERSATION` → requires `messages`
- `record_type: QUERY` → requires `query`

### Evaluators (Agent-Like Entities)

Evaluators act as specialized agents that measure and judge other agents' performance. Each evaluator combines:

1. **Rubric Definition**: Metrics and scoring criteria (what to measure)
2. **Access Control**: Admins and users with actor claims (who can use the evaluator)
3. **Execution Configuration**: Model, timeout, retry settings (how to execute)

**Important - External Permissions Required:**
For evaluators to execute jobs, they need access to external resources:
- **Agent Service**: Share target agents with evaluator's `created_by` actor (add to agent's `users` list)
- **KB Service**: Share KB libraries with evaluator's `created_by` actor (grant library access)

These permissions are managed in the respective services, not in the Eval Service.

**System vs User Evaluators:**
- **System Evaluators**: Pre-defined, Trimble-managed evaluator templates (e.g., faithfulness, safety)
  - Read-only, cannot be modified or used directly for jobs
  - Must be **cloned** to create a user-owned instance
  - Managed by `accounts:trimble-placeholder-of-employees`

- **User Evaluators**: User-owned instances (created from scratch or cloned from system templates)
  - Can be customized and used for job execution
  - Creator becomes admin automatically

### Evaluation Jobs

An "Evaluation Job" coordinates evaluation work (automated or manual) against a dataset:

**Executor Types:**

**AUTOMATED** - LLM evaluator runs automatically:
- Creates via `POST /jobs` with `executor_type: AUTOMATED`
- LLM evaluator executes against all records
- Creates RecordEvaluation entries automatically
- Status: QUEUED → PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED/FAILED/CANCELLED
- Use for: Bulk evaluation, regression testing, CI/CD pipelines

**MANUAL** - Human evaluators submit manually:
- Creates via `POST /jobs` with `executor_type: MANUAL` and `assigned_evaluators`
- Job acts as a container for human evaluation work
- Humans submit evaluations with this job_id
- Job tracks completion as evaluations are submitted
- Status: PENDING → RUNNING (some submitted) → COMPLETED (all submitted)
- Use for: Quality assurance, spot-checking, LLM validation

## Async Execution Model

Evaluation jobs execute asynchronously with a queue-based execution model:

### Job Lifecycle

1. **Create Job**: POST to `/jobs` returns 202 Accepted with initial job object
2. **Queuing**: Job enters a FIFO queue based on its queue key (agent + evaluator + dataset combination)
3. **Execution**: When the job reaches the front of the queue, it moves to PENDING and begins execution
4. **Poll Status**: GET `/jobs/{job_id}` to check progress
5. **Retrieve Results**:
   - GET `/jobs/{job_id}/results` for summary with all record results
   - GET `/results` for individual evaluations

### Job Status Flow

```
QUEUED → PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED
                          ↘ FAILED
         ↓
      CANCELLED (via POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel)
```

- **QUEUED**: Job is waiting in the queue for its turn to execute
- **PENDING**: Job is at the front of the queue and ready to be picked up by a worker
- **RUNNING**: Job is actively being processed by the worker
- **COMPLETED**: Job finished successfully
- **FAILED**: Job failed during execution
- **CANCELLED**: Job was cancelled while in QUEUED status (before execution started)

### Queue Key

Jobs are grouped into queues based on a composite key:
- **Agent ID** (for AGENT jobs)
- **Evaluator ID**
- **Dataset ID**

Only one job per queue key can be active (PENDING or RUNNING) at a time. This prevents resource contention and ensures predictable execution order. When a job completes, the next QUEUED job in the same queue automatically starts.

### Execution Engine

The execution engine:
- Processes records in batches from the dataset
- Creates a `RecordEvaluation` entry for each record
- Invokes the target agent (evaluator must have agent access granted in Agent Service)
- Applies the evaluator's rubric to generate scores
- Aggregates metrics against success criteria
- Generates pass/fail outcome for the job

**Connection**: Each job creates multiple `RecordEvaluation` entries, linked via `job_id`

### Job Cancellation

Jobs that are waiting in the queue (status: `QUEUED`) can be cancelled:

- **Endpoint**: `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel`
- **Permission**: Only admins of the evaluator can cancel jobs
- **Constraint**: Only jobs in `QUEUED` status can be cancelled
- **Result**: Job status changes to `CANCELLED` and is removed from the queue

Jobs that have already started execution (`PENDING`, `RUNNING`) cannot be cancelled. This ensures that partially completed work is not abandoned.

## Error Handling

### Handling Errors and Retry Logic

Common HTTP Response Status Codes are used.
The response body for all error responses are in the same format and include the error code and message.

When you send requests to and get responses from the Service API, you might encounter two types of API errors

- **Client errors** - Client errors are indicated by a 4xx HTTP response code. Client errors indicate
that the service found a problem with the client request. This can include such things as an authentication
failure or missing required parameters. Fix the issue in the client application before submitting the request again.
- **Server errors** - Server errors are indicated by a 5xx HTTP response code and need to be resolved by Trimble.
You may resubmit/retry the request until it succeeds.

For each API error, the API Gateway returns the following values

- A status code, for example, `400`
- An error type, for example, `VALIDATION_ERROR`
- An error message, for example, `The request contains invalid parameters`

### Error Response Body Schema

All error responses follow the same structure for the response body and are conformant to [RFC 9457](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html) specification.

| Error type                    | HTTP Status Code | Error description |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------- |
| `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`       | 500              | The internal server error, please try again later. |
| `DATABASE_OPERATION_FAILED`   | 500              | Database operation failed. |
| `RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND`          | 404              | The requested resource was not found. |
| `UNAUTHORIZED`                | 401              | Authentication is required to access this resource. |
| `PERMISSION_DENIED`           | 403              | Permission denied for the requested resource. |
| `VALIDATION_ERROR`            | 422              | Request validation failed. |
| `BAD_REQUEST`                 | 400              | The request contains invalid parameters. |
| `INVALID_URL`                 | 404              | The requested URL was not found on this server. |
| `HTTP_ERROR`                  | varies           | Generic HTTP error. |
| `UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT`       | 422              | The request is well-formed but cannot be processed. |

### Error Status Codes (Client and Server Errors)

HTTP status codes indicate whether an operation is successful or not.
A response code of 2xx indicates that the operation was successful. Other error codes indicate either a
client error (4xx) or a server error (5xx).
The error table lists the errors returned by the service. Some errors are resolved if you retry the same request.
As a general rule, the timeout errors, network errors, throttling errors, and 5xx errors may be retried.
The 401 error code usually indicates expired credentials and should be retried after refreshing the access token.
The error table indicates which errors are likely to be resolved with successive retries.

If the value of the Retry column is

- **Yes** - Submit the same request again.
- **Yes if idempotent** - Submit the same request again, but only if the given method has been
implemented with idempotence.
- **No** - Fix the problem on the client-side before submitting a new request.

For more information about retrying requests, see Error Retries and Exponential Backoff.

| HTTP Status Code | Error type class                      | Retry                                             |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| 400              | Bad Request Exception                 | No                                                |
| 401              | Unauthorized Exception                | No, could be retried after acquiring a new token  |
| 403              | Access Denied Exception               | No                                                |
| 404              | Not Found Exception                   | No                                                |
| 422              | Unprocessable Entity Exception        | No                                                |
| 429              | Too Many Requests Exception           | Yes                                               |
| 500              | Internal Service Failure Exception    | Yes                                               |
| 502              | Bad Gateway Exception                 | Yes if idempotent                                 |
| 503              | Service Unavailable Exception         | Yes                                               |
| 504              | Endpoint Request Timed-out Exception  | Yes if idempotent                                 |

### Catching Errors

For your application to run smoothly, you need to build in logic to catch and respond to errors.
One typical approach is to implement your request within a try block or if-then statement.
The client-side SDKs typically perform retries and error checking out of the box.

The error code and description are always send in the request response body in addition to HTTP status code.
To troubleshoot the problem please capture the request identifier using the `x-request-id` HTTP response header. Inform the value received in this header to support team.

### Error Retries and Exponential Backoff

Numerous components on a network, such as DNS servers, switches, load balancers,
and others can generate errors anywhere in the life of a given request.

The usual technique for dealing with these error responses in a networked environment
is to implement retries in the client application.
This technique increases the reliability of the application and reduces operational costs for the developer.

If you're not using the official SDKs, you should retry original requests that receive server errors (5xx).
However, client errors (4xx, other than a TooManyRequestsException) indicate you need to
revise the request itself to correct the problem before trying again.

In addition to simple retries, it is recommended to use an exponential backoff algorithm for better flow control.
The idea behind exponential backoff is to use progressively longer waits between retries
for consecutive error responses.
For example, you might let one second elapse
before the first retry, four seconds before the second retry, 16 seconds before the third retry, and so on.
However, if the request has not succeeded after a minute, the problem might be a hard limit and not the
request rate.
Set the maximum number of retries to stop around one minute.

## Payload compression

The service accepts `gzip` and `br` compressed request payloads. The `Content-Encoding` header must be sent in the request
to decode the request payload properly.

The service supports the following content encodings for responses:

- `gzip`
- `br`
- `identity`

Only responses larger than 1KB might be encoded. Small responses are sent without encoding.

The encoding (compression) method can be negotiated with the `Accept-Encoding` header.
The following `Accept-Encoding` header formats, according to the [RFC 7231](<https://tools.ietf.org/html/>
rfc7231#section-5.3.4) specification, are supported:

- `Accept-Encoding:br,gzip`
- `Accept-Encoding:` - If the Accept-Encoding header is present and empty, only the unencoded content is acceptable.
- `Accept-Encoding:*` - This is the default value if the header is not present. This directive does not suggest that any algorithm is supported but indicates that no preference is expressed. Service will decide on the best compression method.
- `Accept-Encoding:br;q=0.5,gzip=1.0`
- `Accept-Encoding:gzip;q=1.0,identity;q=0.5,*;q=0`

If the header is not set, the default value is `*` as defined in RFC 7231. In such cases, the payload is compressed with best supported compression method.

## Limits

The service has the following limits:

- Maximum evaluation job duration: 2 hours
- Maximum dataset size: 10,000 records
- Maximum concurrent jobs per user: 10
- Request rate limit: 100 requests per minute per user
- Stuck job threshold: 1 hour (jobs in RUNNING status longer than this are automatically marked as FAILED)

## Background Processing

The service includes automatic background processes to ensure reliable job execution:

### Stuck Job Cleanup

A periodic task runs every 10 minutes to detect and recover stuck jobs:
- **Detection**: Identifies jobs that have been in `RUNNING` status for more than 1 hour
- **Recovery**: Marks stuck jobs as `FAILED` with an appropriate error message
- **Queue Progression**: Triggers the next `QUEUED` job in the same queue to start execution

### Automatic Job Triggering

When a job completes (successfully or with failure):
1. The completed job is removed from the active queue
2. The next `QUEUED` job in the same queue (same agent + evaluator + dataset) is automatically activated
3. The newly activated job moves to `PENDING` status and begins execution

This ensures continuous processing of queued jobs without manual intervention.

## Success Criteria

Runs use programmatic rules to determine pass/fail outcomes:
- **metric_name**: The metric to evaluate (e.g., "faithfulness")
- **function**: Aggregation function (avg, count, min, max, p99)
- **operator**: Comparison operator (gte, lte, gt, lt, eq)
- **threshold**: Target value
- **value**: (Optional) For categorical metrics with count function

Example: `avg(faithfulness) >= 0.98` means the average faithfulness score must be at least 98%.

Evaluator.title: Evaluator
Evaluator.description:
An evaluator acts as an agent that can execute evaluation jobs.

It has a rubric (metrics), execution configuration (model, permissions),
and can be invoked against datasets.

System vs User Evaluators:
- System-managed evaluators (`is_system_managed: true`) are read-only templates
- User evaluators can be created from scratch or cloned from system templates
- Only user evaluators can be used for job creation

Access Control: Similar to agents.yaml, evaluators have admins and users
with role-based permissions.

name.title: Name
name.description: (Optional) Name for the cloned evaluator. If not provided, defaults to "{original_name} (Copy)".

admins.title: Admins
admins.description: (Optional) Admins for the cloned evaluator. If not provided, creator becomes sole admin.

users.title: Users
users.description: (Optional) Users who can create jobs with the cloned evaluator. Use claims like "users:*", "accounts:engineering"

executionConfig.description: (Optional) Override execution behavior configuration. If not provided, inherits from source template.

EvaluatorClone.title: EvaluatorClone
EvaluatorClone.description:
Payload for cloning an evaluator (typically a system template).

Creates a new user-owned evaluator with `is_system_managed: false`.
The cloned evaluator can be customized after creation.

modelId.title: Modelid
modelId.description: The LLM model to use for LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations (e.g., 'gpt-4', 'claude-3-opus')

timeoutSeconds.title: Timeoutseconds
timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time allowed for evaluating a single record

retryConfig.description: Retry configuration for handling transient failures

searchType.title: Searchtype
searchType.description: Type of search for KB retrieval: 'hybrid', 'semantic', or 'fulltext'. Default is 'hybrid' (recommended for most use cases)

topK.title: Topk
topK.description: Number of chunks to retrieve from KB Service. Maximum is 50. Default is 10.

scoreThreshold.title: Scorethreshold
scoreThreshold.description: Minimum score threshold for retrieved chunks (0.0-1.0). If set, only chunks with score >= threshold are returned.

searchMode.title: Searchmode
searchMode.description: Search mode for fulltext search: 'all' (AND logic) or 'any' (OR logic). Note: Currently not supported by KB Service and is ignored.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: FIQL filter expression to limit search scope based on metadata. Example: tags=in=(product:tekla-structures;version:2025)

EvaluatorExecutionConfig.title: EvaluatorExecutionConfig
EvaluatorExecutionConfig.description:
Configuration for evaluator execution behavior.

Includes model selection, timeouts, retry logic, and retrieval parameters.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: Proportion of records requiring multi-step reasoning

multicontext.title: Multicontext
multicontext.description: Proportion of records requiring information from multiple contexts

comparative.title: Comparative
comparative.description: Proportion of records requiring comparison between entities

concretizing.title: Concretizing
concretizing.description: Proportion of records making abstract concepts more concrete

EvolutionMix.title: EvolutionMix
EvolutionMix.description: Distribution of evolution strategies for generating diverse test cases.

chunkId.title: Chunkid
chunkId.description: The unique identifier of the chunk from KB Service

relevance.title: Relevance
relevance.description: Importance level: critical, important, or supplementary

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why this chunk is expected (for documentation/debugging)

text.title: Text
text.description: The chunk text (for human readability when reviewing records)

ExpectedChunk.title: ExpectedChunk
ExpectedChunk.description:
Defines an expected KB chunk that should be retrieved for a query.

Used as ground truth for retrieval evaluation.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the tool to be called

inputParameters.title: Inputparameters
inputParameters.description: The expected input parameters for the tool

expectedResponse.title: Expectedresponse
expectedResponse.description: The mocked JSON response to be returned by the tool

persona.title: Persona
persona.description: The persona/role the LLM should adopt

inputStyle.title: Inputstyle
inputStyle.description: The style/format of generated user inputs

outputStyle.title: Outputstyle
outputStyle.description: The style/format of generated expected outputs

GenerationPrompts.title: GenerationPrompts
GenerationPrompts.description: Prompt templates that guide the LLM-as-a-Curator.

actors.title: Actors
actors.description: Actor claims defining who executes this job

JobAssignee.title: JobAssignee
JobAssignee.description:
Unified assignee model using actor claims.

Actor types:
- evaluator:{uuid} - LLM evaluator agent (AUTOMATED execution)
- users:{identifier} - Specific user (MANUAL execution)
- accounts:{name} - All users in an account (MANUAL execution)
- applications:{name} - Specific application (MANUAL execution)

JobOutcome.description: Job outcome enumeration for evaluation jobs.

KBLibraryInfo.title: KBLibraryInfo
KBLibraryInfo.description: Nested KB library information in job response.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the message sender

content.title: Content
content.description: Plain text or multi-modal content array

Message.title: Message
Message.description:
A message in the conversation history.

Supports multi-modal content (text, images, audio, video).

generatedImages.title: Generatedimages
generatedImages.description: Array of generated images with url, prompt, revised_prompt

generatedAudio.title: Generatedaudio
generatedAudio.description: Array of generated audio files with url, format, voice, duration

generatedVideo.title: Generatedvideo
generatedVideo.description: Array of generated videos with url, format, duration

MultiModalOutput.title: MultiModalOutput
MultiModalOutput.description:
Multi-modal output that can contain text, generated images, audio, or video.

Used for agents that generate images (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion)
or synthesize speech.

description.description: A brief description of what the metric measures

OutputMetric.description: Definition of a metric that an evaluator produces.

RecordCreateRequest.title: RecordCreateRequest
RecordCreateRequest.description: Request body for creating one or more records.

id.title: Id
id.description: [Primary Key] Unique identifier for this evaluation

recordId.title: Recordid
recordId.description: The dataset record that was evaluated

datasetId.title: Datasetid
datasetId.description: The dataset this record belongs to

sourceType.description: Whether this evaluation was performed by an LLM or human

evaluatorId.title: Evaluatorid
evaluatorId.description: The evaluator (agent) that performed or defined this evaluation

evaluatorActor.title: Evaluatoractor
evaluatorActor.description: The actor who performed the evaluation. Auto-populated from OAuth token for HUMAN evaluations. System-managed for LLM evaluations.

jobId.title: Jobid
jobId.description: The evaluation job this evaluation is associated with. Context inherited from job: agent_id, agent_version, kb_library_versions, model_id. Links to GET /jobs/{job_id} for full job context.

traceId.title: Traceid
traceId.description: Reference to the execution trace. For EVALUATE_AGENT jobs: agent_trace_id (from Agent Service). For EVALUATE_RETRIEVAL jobs: search_trace_id (from KB Service). The trace contains: actual_output, execution details, tool calls, timing.

agentId.title: Agentid
agentId.description: The agent that was evaluated. Auto-populated from job.

agentVersion.title: Agentversion
agentVersion.description: The version number of the agent that was evaluated. Auto-populated from job.

kbLibraryVersions.title: Kblibraryversions
kbLibraryVersions.description: Snapshot of KB library versions at evaluation time. Auto-populated from job. Map of library_id -> version_number.

modelId.title: Modelid
modelId.description: The model used for evaluation. Auto-populated from job (from evaluator's execution_config).

actualOutput.title: Actualoutput
actualOutput.description: The output that was evaluated. Auto-fetched from trace_id. For EVALUATE_AGENT jobs: Retrieved from agent trace. For EVALUATE_RETRIEVAL jobs: Retrieved from search trace.

actualTools.title: Actualtools
actualTools.description: The actual tool calls made by the agent during evaluation. Each tool call includes the tool name, input parameters, and response.

actualContext.title: Actualcontext
actualContext.description: The actual chunks retrieved by the agent

expectedOutput.title: Expectedoutput
expectedOutput.description: The ground-truth ideal final answer from the dataset record

expectedContext.title: Expectedcontext
expectedContext.description: The ground-truth ideal KB chunks from the dataset record

expectedTools.title: Expectedtools
expectedTools.description: The ground-truth ideal tool calls from the dataset record

scores.title: Scores
scores.description: Map of metric_name to score value. Example: {"faithfulness": 0.95, "relevance": 0.88}

comment.title: Comment
comment.description: (Optional) Comments or notes from the evaluator

evaluatedAt.title: Evaluatedat
evaluatedAt.description: Timestamp when this evaluation was performed

createdBy.title: Createdby
createdBy.description: The user CURIE who created the evaluation

updatedBy.title: Updatedby
updatedBy.description: The user CURIE who last updated the evaluation

accountId.title: Accountid
accountId.description: The account ID associated with this evaluation

RecordEvaluation.title: RecordEvaluation
RecordEvaluation.description:
A single evaluation (human or LLM) for a specific record.

Connection to Jobs: When an evaluator job executes, it creates individual
RecordEvaluation entries for each record in the dataset. The `job_id` field
links back to the job that created this evaluation.

Context Tracking: Captures agent version, KB library versions, and model used
to enable accurate comparison and regression testing.

datasetId.title: Datasetid
datasetId.description: [Required] The dataset this record belongs to

evaluatorId.title: Evaluatorid
evaluatorId.description: [Required] The evaluator (rubric) to use for scoring. Should match the evaluator used by the job for consistency.

jobId.title: Jobid
jobId.description: [Required] The job whose output is being reviewed. Auto-populates: agent_id, agent_version, kb_library_versions, model_id

traceId.title: Traceid
traceId.description: [Required] The execution trace ID. For EVALUATE_AGENT jobs: agent_trace_id from Agent Service. For EVALUATE_RETRIEVAL jobs: search_trace_id from KB Service. Auto-fetches: actual_output

scores.title: Scores
scores.description: [Required] Map of metric_name to score value. Must include all metrics defined in the evaluator's output_metrics. Example: {"faithfulness": 0.95, "relevance": 0.88, "safety": true}

comment.title: Comment
comment.description: (Optional) Comments or notes about the evaluation. Recommended for explaining scores, especially when disagreeing with LLM evaluation.

RecordEvaluationSubmit.title: RecordEvaluationSubmit
RecordEvaluationSubmit.description:
Payload for submitting a new evaluation for a record.

What you provide (6 fields):
- dataset_id, record_id, evaluator_id, job_id, trace_id, scores

What API auto-populates (9 fields):
- evaluator_actor (from OAuth token)
- agent_id, agent_version, kb_library_versions, model_id (from job)
- actual_output (from trace)
- source_type, evaluated_at, created_at (system-managed)

recordType.description: Update the record type (changes which fields are used)

query.title: Query
query.description: (For QUERY records) Update the search query

expectedOutput.title: Expectedoutput
expectedOutput.description: The 'ground truth' ideal final answer from the agent

expectedContext.title: Expectedcontext
expectedContext.description: The 'ground truth' ideal KB chunks that should be retrieved

expectedTools.title: Expectedtools
expectedTools.description: The 'ground truth' ideal tools the agent should have called

referenceOutput.title: Referenceoutput
referenceOutput.description: A 'blessed' output from a past run for regression testing

context.title: Context
context.description: Optional run context items forwarded to the agent's `context` parameter when the record is evaluated

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: A flexible JSON object for functional, searchable data

RecordUpdateRequest.description:
Fields that can be updated on a DatasetRecord.

Typically for HITL workflows or storing reference run results.

retrievedChunks.title: Retrievedchunks
retrievedChunks.description: Array of retrieved chunks in ranked order

retrievalParams.title: Retrievalparams
retrievalParams.description: Parameters used for retrieval (top, score_threshold, libraries)

RetrievalResult.title: RetrievalResult
RetrievalResult.description:
Results from a KB Service retrieval query.

Contains the chunks that were retrieved, with their ranking and scores.

maxRetries.title: Maxretries
maxRetries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts on transient failures

backoffMultiplier.title: Backoffmultiplier
backoffMultiplier.description: Multiplier for exponential backoff between retries

RetryConfig.title: RetryConfig
RetryConfig.description: Retry configuration for handling transient failures during evaluation.

description.title: Description
description.description: Unique description/key for the context item

value.title: Value
value.description: String value for the context item

RunContext.title: RunContext
RunContext.description:
A single context item forwarded to the agent's `context` parameter.

Mirrors the Trimble agent SDK's `Context` type: `description` and `value`
are required, and additional arbitrary properties are allowed and preserved.

value.title: Value
value.description: The specific categorical value to filter on for a count function

SuccessCriterion.title: SuccessCriterion
SuccessCriterion.description: A rule that defines a pass/fail condition for a single metric.

statistic.title: Statistic
statistic.description: The calculated statistic or categorical statistic

SummaryResult-Input.title: SummaryResult
SummaryResult-Input.description: The calculated result for a single success criterion.

statistic.title: Statistic
statistic.description: The calculated statistic or categorical statistic

SummaryResult-Output.title: SummaryResult
SummaryResult-Output.description: The calculated result for a single success criterion.

kbLibraryLinks.title: Kblibrarylinks
kbLibraryLinks.description: An updated array of KB Service library UUIDs

outputMetrics.title: Outputmetrics
outputMetrics.description: An updated list of metrics this evaluator produces

executionConfig.description: Updated execution behavior configuration (model, timeout, retry)

UpdateEvaluatorRequest.title: UpdateEvaluatorRequest
UpdateEvaluatorRequest.description:
Payload for updating a user-owned evaluator.

Only admins can update evaluators.
Fails if `is_system_managed: true` (system evaluators are read-only).

tags.name: Datasets
tags.description: Endpoints for managing datasets and their records. Datasets serve as containers grouping related test cases, while dataset records represent individual test cases withinput data, expected outputs, and ground truth for evaluation.

tags.name: Evaluators
tags.description: Endpoints for managing evaluator agents (rubrics, permissions, and execution configuration).

tags.name: Evaluation Jobs
tags.description: Endpoints for managing evaluation jobs (top-level resource). Jobs execute evaluators against datasets asynchronously, creating RecordEvaluation entries for each record.

tags.name: Evaluation Results
tags.description: Endpoints for managing evaluation results with flexible filtering by dataset, record, job, agent, evaluator, and actor. Stores both human and LLM evaluations. Enables cross-dataset analysis and inter-rater reliability comparisons.
